stem: A loan application enters the loan processing pipeline. The probability of passing application review is 97%. If the previous stage is passed, the probability of passing credit check is 91%. If the previous stage is passed, the probability of passing underwriting is between 70% and 86% (uncertain due to evaluator variability). What is the overall probability that the loan application is approved?

ground_truth.answer: P(approved) ranges from 0.618 to 0.759 as the underwriting pass rate varies from 70% to 86%. Point estimate: 0.689.

solution_steps: The loan application must pass all 3 stages sequentially: P(success) = P(stage 1)×P(stage 2)×P(stage 3).
The uncertainty in the underwriting stage creates a 14.1 percentage point range in the overall success probability.
common_failure_modes: Adding probabilities instead of multiplying for sequential stages
Using a single value for the underwriting rate without acknowledging uncertainty
Not recognizing that uncertainty at one stage propagates through all subsequent stages
